name: Ahrefs
one_liner: SEO toolset whose API v3 exposes Site Explorer, Keywords Explorer, backlinks, rank tracker and site audit data for agents.
access_notes: API v3 is available only on Lite or higher paid plans; each plan includes a monthly API-unit budget (Lite 100k up to Enterprise 2M). No free tier or trial for the full API; a limited set of free test queries exists for development, but real data endpoints consume units and require an active paid subscription. API keys are created by workspace owners/admins in Account settings.
api_notes: REST API v3 with an OpenAPI-compatible spec, organized into ~11 endpoint groups (Site Explorer, Keywords Explorer, Site Audit, SERP Overview, Rank Tracker, Batch Analysis, Brand Radar, Social Media Management, Management, Subscription Information, Public). Rate limited to 60 requests/minute (HTTP 429 on excess).
mcp_notes: Ahrefs ships an official remote (hosted) MCP server at https://api.ahrefs.com/mcp/mcp using OAuth, available to Lite-plan and higher users (github.com/ahrefs/ahrefs-mcp-server). A legacy local server using API v3 keys exists but is unmaintained and not recommended.
main_blocker: API requires a paid Lite+ subscription (no trial/sandbox path to working credentials); only limited free test queries are available.

evidence.claim: API v3 uses API key auth (created by workspace owners/admins), is REST with OpenAPI-compatible spec, and covers 11 endpoint groups; 60 req/min rate limit.
API v3 is available only for Lite and higher subscription plans; plans include monthly API-unit budgets (Lite 100k to Enterprise 2M), minimum 50 units per request, no free tier.
Ahrefs ships an official MCP server (remote hosted at api.ahrefs.com/mcp, OAuth-based; legacy local server unmaintained).

open_questions: Exact scope/limits of the free test queries (whether they yield agent-usable data), and whether an API key can be generated on a non-paid account before activation.
